member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.ExperimentalAttribute
member.summary: Indicates that an API is experimental and it may change in the future.
member.remarks: This attribute allows call sites to be flagged with a diagnostic that indicates that an experimental feature is used. Authors can use this attribute to ship preview features in their assemblies.

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.ExperimentalAttribute.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class, specifying the ID that the compiler will use when reporting a use of the API the attribute applies to.
member.param: The ID that the compiler will use when reporting a use of the API the attribute applies to.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.ExperimentalAttribute.DiagnosticId
member.summary: Gets the ID that the compiler will use when reporting a use of the API the attribute applies to.
member.remarks:
The diagnostic ID is shown in build output for warnings and errors.
This property represents the unique ID that can be used to suppress the warnings or errors, if needed.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.ExperimentalAttribute.UrlFormat
member.summary: Gets or sets the URL for corresponding documentation. The API accepts a format string instead of an actual URL, creating a generic URL that includes the diagnostic ID.
member.value: The format string that represents a URL to corresponding documentation.
member.remarks: An example format string is https://contoso.com/obsoletion-warnings/{0}.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.RequiresDynamicCodeAttribute
member.summary: Indicates that the specified method requires the ability to generate new code at runtime, for example through .
member.remarks: This allows tools to understand which methods are unsafe to call when compiling ahead of time.

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.RequiresDynamicCodeAttribute.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with the specified message.
member.param: A message that contains information about the usage of dynamic code.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.RequiresDynamicCodeAttribute.Message
member.summary: Gets a message that contains information about the usage of dynamic code.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.RequiresDynamicCodeAttribute.Url
member.summary: Gets or sets an optional URL that contains more information about the method, why it requires dynamic code, and what options a consumer has to deal with it.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.RequiresUnreferencedCodeAttribute
member.summary: Indicates that the specified method requires dynamic access to code that is not referenced statically, for example through .
member.remarks: This allows tools to understand which methods are unsafe to call when removing unreferenced code from an application.

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.RequiresUnreferencedCodeAttribute.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with the specified message.
member.param: A message that contains information about the usage of unreferenced code.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.RequiresUnreferencedCodeAttribute.Message
member.summary: Gets a message that contains information about the usage of unreferenced code.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.RequiresUnreferencedCodeAttribute.Url
member.summary: Gets or sets an optional URL that contains more information about the method, why it requires unreferenced code, and what options a consumer has to deal with it.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.UnconditionalSuppressMessageAttribute
member.summary: Suppresses reporting of a specific rule violation, allowing multiple suppressions on a single code artifact.
member.remarks: is different than in that it doesn't have a . So it is always preserved in the compiled assembly.

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.UnconditionalSuppressMessageAttribute.#ctor(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class, specifying the category of the tool and the identifier for an analysis rule.
member.param: The category for the attribute.
The identifier of the analysis rule the attribute applies to.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.UnconditionalSuppressMessageAttribute.Category
member.summary: Gets the category identifying the classification of the attribute.
member.remarks: The property describes the tool or tool analysis category for which a message suppression attribute applies.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.UnconditionalSuppressMessageAttribute.CheckId
member.summary: Gets the identifier of the analysis tool rule to be suppressed.
member.remarks: Concatenated together, the and properties form a unique check identifier.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.UnconditionalSuppressMessageAttribute.Scope
member.summary: Gets or sets the scope of the code that is relevant for the attribute.
member.remarks: The Scope property is an optional argument that specifies the metadata scope for which the attribute is relevant.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.UnconditionalSuppressMessageAttribute.Target
member.summary: Gets or sets a fully qualified path that represents the target of the attribute.
member.remarks: The property is an optional argument identifying the analysis target of the attribute. An example value is "System.IO.Stream.ctor():System.Void". Because it is fully qualified, it can be long, particularly for targets such as parameters. The analysis tool user interface should be capable of automatically formatting the parameter.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.UnconditionalSuppressMessageAttribute.MessageId
member.summary: Gets or sets an optional argument expanding on exclusion criteria.
member.remarks: The property is an optional argument that specifies additional exclusion where the literal metadata target is not sufficiently precise. For example, the cannot be applied within a method, and it may be desirable to suppress a violation against a statement in the method that will give a rule violation, but not against all statements in the method.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.UnconditionalSuppressMessageAttribute.Justification
member.summary: Gets or sets the justification for suppressing the code analysis message.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicDependencyAttribute
member.summary: States a dependency that one member has on another.
member.remarks: This can be used to inform tooling of a dependency that is otherwise not evident purely from metadata and IL, for example a member relied on via reflection.

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicDependencyAttribute.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with the specified signature of a member on the same type as the consumer.
member.param: The signature of the member depended on.

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicDependencyAttribute.#ctor(System.String,System.Type)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with the specified signature of a member on a .
member.param: The signature of the member depended on.
The containing .

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicDependencyAttribute.#ctor(System.String,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with the specified signature of a member on a type in an assembly.
member.param: The signature of the member depended on.
The full name of the type containing the specified member.
The assembly name of the type containing the specified member.

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicDependencyAttribute.#ctor(System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes,System.Type)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with the specified types of members on a .
member.param: The types of members depended on.
The containing the specified members.

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicDependencyAttribute.#ctor(System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes,System.String,System.String)

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicDependencyAttribute.MemberSignature
member.summary: Gets the signature of the member depended on.
member.remarks: Either must be a valid string or must not equal , but not both.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicDependencyAttribute.MemberTypes
member.summary: Gets the which specifies the type of members depended on.
member.remarks: Either must be a valid string or must not equal , but not both.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicDependencyAttribute.Type
member.summary: Gets the containing the specified member.
member.remarks: If neither nor are specified, the type of the consumer is assumed.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicDependencyAttribute.TypeName
member.summary: Gets the full name of the type containing the specified member.
member.remarks: If neither nor are specified, the type of the consumer is assumed.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicDependencyAttribute.AssemblyName
member.summary: Gets the assembly name of the specified type.
member.remarks: is only valid when is specified.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicDependencyAttribute.Condition
member.summary: Gets or sets the condition in which the dependency is applicable, e.g. "DEBUG".

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMembersAttribute
member.summary: Indicates that certain members on a specified are accessed dynamically, for example through .
member.remarks:
This allows tools to understand which members are being accessed during the execution of a program.
This attribute is valid on members whose type is or .
When this attribute is applied to a location of type , the assumption is that the string represents a fully qualified type name.
When this attribute is applied to a class, interface, or struct, the members specified can be accessed dynamically on instances returned from calling on instances of that class, interface, or struct.
If the attribute is applied to a method it's treated as a special case and it implies the attribute should be applied to the "this" parameter of the method. As such the attribute should only be used on instance methods of types assignable to System.Type (or string, but no methods will use it there).

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMembersAttribute.#ctor(System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with the specified member types.
member.param: The types of members dynamically accessed.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMembersAttribute.MemberTypes
member.summary: Gets the which specifies the type of members dynamically accessed.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes
member.summary:
Specifies the types of members that are dynamically accessed.
This enumeration has a attribute that allows a bitwise combination of its member values.

member.name: T:Azure.Provisioning.Resources.AzureCliScript
member.summary: AzureCliScript.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Resources.AzureCliScript.AzCliVersion
member.summary: Azure CLI module version to be used.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Resources.AzureCliScript.RetentionInterval
member.summary: Interval for which the service retains the script resource after it reaches a terminal state. Resource will be deleted when this duration expires. Duration is based on ISO 8601 pattern (for example P1D means one day).

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Resources.AzureCliScript.Arguments
member.summary: Command line arguments to pass to the script. Arguments are separated by spaces. ex: -Name blue* -Location 'West US 2'.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Resources.AzureCliScript.CleanupPreference
member.summary: The clean up preference when the script execution gets in a terminal state. Default setting is 'Always'.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Resources.AzureCliScript.ContainerGroupName
member.summary: Container group name, if not specified then the name will get auto-generated. Not specifying a 'containerGroupName' indicates the system to generate a unique name which might end up flagging an Azure Policy as non-compliant. Use 'containerGroupName' when you have an Azure Policy that expects a specific naming convention or when you want to fully control the name. 'containerGroupName' property must be between 1 and 63 characters long, must contain only lowercase letters, numbers, and dashes and it cannot start or end with a dash and consecutive dashes are not allowed. To specify a 'containerGroupName', add the following object to properties: { "containerSettings": { "containerGroupName": "contoso-container" } }. If you do not want to specify a 'containerGroupName' then do not add 'containerSettings' property.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Resources.AzureCliScript.ContainerSettings
member.summary: Container settings.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Resources.AzureCliScript.EnvironmentVariables
member.summary: The environment variables to pass over to the script.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Resources.AzureCliScript.ForceUpdateTag
member.summary: Gets or sets how the deployment script should be forced to execute even if the script resource has not changed. Can be current time stamp or a GUID.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Resources.AzureCliScript.PrimaryScriptUri
member.summary: Uri for the script. This is the entry point for the external script.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Resources.AzureCliScript.ScriptContent
member.summary: Script body.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Resources.AzureCliScript.Timeout
member.summary: Maximum allowed script execution time specified in ISO 8601 format. Default value is P1D.

To assign an already formatted json string to this property use System.BinaryData.FromString(System.String). Examples: BinaryData.FromObjectAsJson("foo")Creates a payload of "foo".BinaryData.FromString("\"foo\"")Creates a payload of "foo".BinaryData.FromObjectAsJson(new { key = "value" })Creates a payload of { "key": "value" }.BinaryData.FromString("{\"key\": \"value\"}")Creates a payload of { "key": "value" }.

member.name: T:Azure.Provisioning.Resources.DeploymentStack
member.summary: DeploymentStack.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Resources.DeploymentStack.Name
member.summary: Name of the deployment stack.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Resources.DeploymentStack.ActionOnUnmanage
member.summary: Defines the behavior of resources that are no longer managed after the Deployment stack is updated or deleted.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Resources.DeploymentStack.BypassStackOutOfSyncError
member.summary: Flag to bypass service errors that indicate the stack resource list is not correctly synchronized.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Resources.DeploymentStack.DebugSettingDetailLevel
member.summary: Specifies the type of information to log for debugging. The permitted values are none, requestContent, responseContent, or both requestContent and responseContent separated by a comma. The default is none. When setting this value, carefully consider the type of information that is being passed in during deployment. By logging information about the request or response, sensitive data that is retrieved through the deployment operations could potentially be exposed.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Resources.DeploymentStack.DenySettings
member.summary: Defines how resources deployed by the stack are locked.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Resources.DeploymentStack.DeploymentScope
member.summary: The scope at which the initial deployment should be created. If a scope is not specified, it will default to the scope of the deployment stack. Valid scopes are: management group (format: '/providers/Microsoft.Management/managementGroups/{managementGroupId}'), subscription (format: '/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}'), resource group (format: '/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/resourceGroups/{resourceGroupName}').

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Resources.DeploymentStack.Description
member.summary: Deployment stack description. Max length of 4096 characters.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Resources.DeploymentStack.Error
member.summary: The error detail.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Resources.DeploymentStack.Location
member.summary: The location of the Deployment stack. It cannot be changed after creation. It must be one of the supported Azure locations.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Resources.DeploymentStack.Parameters
member.summary: Name and value pairs that define the deployment parameters for the template. Use this element when providing the parameter values directly in the request, rather than linking to an existing parameter file. Use either the parametersLink property or the parameters property, but not both.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Resources.DeploymentStack.ParametersLink
member.summary: The URI of parameters file. Use this element to link to an existing parameters file. Use either the parametersLink property or the parameters property, but not both.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Resources.DeploymentStack.Tags
member.summary: Deployment stack resource tags.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Resources.DeploymentStack.Template
member.summary: The template content. You use this element when you want to pass the template syntax directly in the request rather than link to an existing template. It can be a JObject or well-formed JSON string. Use either the templateLink property or the template property, but not both. To assign an object to this property use System.BinaryData.FromObjectAsJson``1(``0,System.Text.Json.JsonSerializerOptions). To assign an already formatted json string to this property use System.BinaryData.FromString(System.String). Examples: BinaryData.FromObjectAsJson("foo")Creates a payload of "foo".BinaryData.FromString("\"foo\"")Creates a payload of "foo".BinaryData.FromObjectAsJson(new { key = "value" })Creates a payload of { "key": "value" }.BinaryData.FromString("{\"key\": \"value\"}")Creates a payload of { "key": "value" }.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Resources.DeploymentStack.TemplateLink
member.summary: The URI of the template. Use either the templateLink property or the template property, but not both.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Resources.DeploymentStack.CorrelationId
member.summary: The correlation id of the last Deployment stack upsert or delete operation. It is in GUID format and is used for tracing.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Resources.DeploymentStack.DeletedResources
member.summary: An array of resources that were deleted during the most recent Deployment stack update. Deleted means that the resource was removed from the template and relevant deletion operations were specified.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Resources.DeploymentStack.DeploymentId
member.summary: The resourceId of the deployment resource created by the deployment stack.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Resources.DeploymentStack.DetachedResources
member.summary: An array of resources that were detached during the most recent Deployment stack update. Detached means that the resource was removed from the template, but no relevant deletion operations were specified. So, the resource still exists while no longer being associated with the stack.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Resources.DeploymentStack.Duration
member.summary: The duration of the last successful Deployment stack update.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Resources.DeploymentStack.FailedResources
member.summary: An array of resources that failed to reach goal state during the most recent update. Each resourceId is accompanied by an error message.

member.summary: Gets the Id.

member.name: T:Azure.Provisioning.Resources.ArmDeploymentMode
member.summary: The mode that is used to deploy resources. This value can be either Incremental or Complete. In Incremental mode, resources are deployed without deleting existing resources that are not included in the template. In Complete mode, resources are deployed and existing resources in the resource group that are not included in the template are deleted. Be careful when using Complete mode as you may unintentionally delete resources.

It can be a JObject or well-formed JSON string. Use either the templateLink property or the template property, but not both. To assign an object to this property use System.BinaryData.FromObjectAsJson``1(``0,System.Text.Json.JsonSerializerOptions). To assign an already formatted json string to this property use System.BinaryData.FromString(System.String). Examples: BinaryData.FromObjectAsJson("foo")Creates a payload of "foo".BinaryData.FromString("\"foo\"")Creates a payload of "foo".BinaryData.FromObjectAsJson(new { key = "value" })Creates a payload of { "key": "value" }.BinaryData.FromString("{\"key\": \"value\"}")Creates a payload of { "key": "value" }.

member.summary: Name and value pairs that define the deployment parameters for the template. You use this element when you want to provide the parameter values directly in the request rather than link to an existing parameter file. Use either the parametersLink property or the parameters property, but not both. It can be a JObject or a well formed JSON string. To assign an object to this property use System.BinaryData.FromObjectAsJson``1(``0,System.Text.Json.JsonSerializerOptions). To assign an already formatted json string to this property use System.BinaryData.FromString(System.String). Examples: BinaryData.FromObjectAsJson("foo")Creates a payload of "foo".BinaryData.FromString("\"foo\"")Creates a payload of "foo".BinaryData.FromObjectAsJson(new { key = "value" })Creates a payload of { "key": "value" }.BinaryData.FromString("{\"key\": \"value\"}")Creates a payload of { "key": "value" }.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Resources.ArmDeploymentProperties.Mode
member.summary: The mode that is used to deploy resources. This value can be either Incremental or Complete. In Incremental mode, resources are deployed without deleting existing resources that are not included in the template. In Complete mode, resources are deployed and existing resources in the resource group that are not included in the template are deleted. Be careful when using Complete mode as you may unintentionally delete resources.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Resources.ArmDeploymentProperties.DebugSettingDetailLevel
member.summary: Specifies the type of information to log for debugging. The permitted values are none, requestContent, responseContent, or both requestContent and responseContent separated by a comma. The default is none. When setting this value, carefully consider the type of information you are passing in during deployment. By logging information about the request or response, you could potentially expose sensitive data that is retrieved through the deployment operations.

member.summary: Defines how resources deployed by the Deployment stack are locked.

member.summary: denySettings Mode that defines denied actions.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Resources.DenySettings.ExcludedPrincipals
member.summary: List of AAD principal IDs excluded from the lock. Up to 5 principals are permitted.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Resources.DenySettings.ExcludedActions
member.summary: List of role-based management operations that are excluded from the denySettings. Up to 200 actions are permitted. If the denySetting mode is set to 'denyWriteAndDelete', then the following actions are automatically appended to 'excludedActions': '*\/read' and 'Microsoft.Authorization/locks/delete'. If the denySetting mode is set to 'denyDelete', then the following actions are automatically appended to 'excludedActions': 'Microsoft.Authorization/locks/delete'. Duplicate actions will be removed.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Resources.DenySettings.ApplyToChildScopes
member.summary: DenySettings will be applied to child resource scopes of every managed resource with a deny assignment.

member.summary: Authorized users are able to read and modify the resources, but cannot delete.

member.summary: Authorized users can read from a resource, but cannot modify or delete it.

member.summary: Authorized users are able to read and modify the resources, but cannot delete.

member.summary: Resource type does not support denyAssignments.

member.summary: denyAssignments are not supported on resources outside the scope of the deployment stack.

member.name: F:Azure.Provisioning.Resources.DenyStatusMode.DenyWriteAndDelete
member.summary: Authorized users can only read from a resource, but cannot modify or delete it.

member.summary: Deny assignment has been removed by Azure due to a resource management change (management group move, etc.).

member.name: T:Azure.Provisioning.Resources.ScriptContainerConfiguration
member.summary: Settings to customize ACI container instance.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Resources.ScriptContainerConfiguration.ContainerGroupName
member.summary: Container group name, if not specified then the name will get auto-generated. Not specifying a 'containerGroupName' indicates the system to generate a unique name which might end up flagging an Azure Policy as non-compliant. Use 'containerGroupName' when you have an Azure Policy that expects a specific naming convention or when you want to fully control the name. 'containerGroupName' property must be between 1 and 63 characters long, must contain only lowercase letters, numbers, and dashes and it cannot start or end with a dash and consecutive dashes are not allowed. To specify a 'containerGroupName', add the following object to properties: { "containerSettings": { "containerGroupName": "contoso-container" } }. If you do not want to specify a 'containerGroupName' then do not add 'containerSettings' property.

member.summary: The policy's excluded scopes.

member.summary: The location of the policy assignment. Only required when utilizing managed identity.

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Resources.PolicyAssignment.Metadata
member.summary: The policy assignment metadata. Metadata is an open ended object and is typically a collection of key value pairs. member.name: T:Azure.Provisioning.Expressions.BicepFunction
member.summary: Provides access to standard Bicep functions.

member.name: M:Azure.Provisioning.Expressions.BicepFunction.GetUniqueString(Azure.Provisioning.BicepValue{System.String}[])
member.summary: Creates a deterministic hash string based on the provided as parameters. This represents the uniqueString Bicep function.
member.param: The value used in the hash function to create a unique string. At least one value is required.

remarks.para: This function is helpful when you need to create a unique name for a resource. You provide parameter values that limit the scope of uniqueness for the result. You can specify whether the name is unique down to subscription, resource group, or deployment.

The returned value isn't a random string, but rather the result of a hash function. The returned value is 13 characters long. It isn't globally unique. You may want to combine the value with a prefix from your naming convention to create a name that is meaningful.

See the
Bicep Functions Reference for more.

member.name: M:Azure.Provisioning.Expressions.BicepFunction.Take(Azure.Provisioning.BicepValue{System.String},Azure.Provisioning.BicepValue{System.Int32})
member.summary: Returns a string with the specified number of characters from the start of the string. This represents the take Bicep function.
member.param: The string to take the elements from.
The number of characters to take. If this value is 0 or less, an empty string is returned. If it's larger than the length of the given string, all the characters in the string are returned.
member.remarks:
See the
Bicep Functions Reference for more.

member.name: M:Azure.Provisioning.Expressions.BicepFunction.CreateGuid(Azure.Provisioning.BicepValue{System.String}[])
member.summary: Creates a value in the format of a globally unique identifier based on the values provided as parameters. This represents the guid Bicep function.
member.param: The value used in the hash function to create the GUID. At least one value is required.
member.returns: A string containing 36 characters in the format of a globally unique identifier.

remarks.para: This function is helpful when you need to create a value in the format of a globally unique identifier. You provide parameter values that limit the scope of uniqueness for the result. You can specify whether the name is unique down to subscription, resource group, or deployment.
The returned value isn't a random string, but rather the result of a hash function on the parameters. The returned value is 36 characters long. It isn't globally unique.
NOTE: The order of the parameters affects the returned value. For example, CreateGuid("Hello", "World") and CreateGuid("World", "Hello") don't return the same value.

See the
Bicep Functions Reference for more.

member.name: M:Azure.Provisioning.Expressions.BicepFunction.GetSubscriptionResourceId(Azure.Provisioning.BicepValue{System.String}[])
member.summary: Returns the unique identifier for a resource deployed at the subscription level. This represents the subscriptionResourceId Bicep function.
member.param: Optional subscription id, resource types, and resource names used to construct the resource ID. At least two values are required.
member.returns: The unique identifier for a resource deployed at the subscription level.

remarks.para: The identifier is returned in the following format: /subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/providers/{resourceProviderNamespace}/{resourceType}/{resourceName}

You use this function to get the resource ID for resources that are deployed to the subscription rather than a resource group. The returned ID differs from the value returned by the resourceId function by not including a resource group value.

See the
Bicep Functions Reference for more.

member.exception: When is empty or contains fewer than two elements.

member.name: M:Azure.Provisioning.Expressions.BicepFunction.GetDeployment
member.summary: Returns information about the current deployment operation. This represents the deployment Bicep function.
member.returns: This function returns the object that is passed during deployment. The properties in the returned object differ based on whether you are deploying a local Bicep file or deploying to a resource group / subscription / management group / tenant.
member.remarks:
See the
Bicep Functions Reference for more.

See the

member.name: P:Azure.Provisioning.Primitives.ProvisionableResource.DependsOn
member.summary: Declares explicit dependencies on other resources.

remarks.para: While you may be inclined to use dependsOn to map relationships between your resources, it's important to understand why you're doing it. For example, to document how resources are interconnected, dependsOn isn't the right approach. After deployment, the resource doesn't retain deployment dependencies in its properties, so there are no commands or operations that let you see dependencies. Setting unnecessary dependencies slows deployment time because Resource Manager can't deploy those resources in parallel.

Even though explicit dependencies are sometimes required, the need for them is rare. In most cases, you can use a symbolic name to imply the dependency between resources. If you find yourself setting explicit dependencies, you should consider if there's a way to remove it.

member.summary: Method that either returns cached value or acquire a lock. If one caller has acquired a lock, other callers will be waiting for the lock to be released. If value is set, lock is released and all waiters get that value. If value isn't set, the next waiter in the queue will get the lock.

Flag controlling if created by this for client method calls should be suppressed when called by other Azure SDK client methods. It's recommended to set it to true for new clients; use default (null) for backward compatibility reasons, or set it to false to explicitly disable suppression for specific cases. The default value could change in the future, the flag should be only set to false if suppression for the client should never be enabled.

member.name: M:Azure.Core.OperationInternal.#ctor(Azure.Core.IOperation,Azure.Core.Pipeline.ClientDiagnostics,Azure.Response,System.String,System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.Collections.Generic.KeyValuePair{System.String,System.String}},Azure.Core.DelayStrategy)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: The long-running operation making use of this class. Passing "this" is expected.
Used for diagnostic scope and exception creation. This is expected to be the instance created during the construction of your main client.

The initial value of . Usually, long-running operation objects can be instantiated in two ways:
When calling a client's "Start<OperationName>" method, a service call is made to start the operation, and an instance is returned. In this case, the response received from this service call can be passed here.
When a user instantiates an directly using a public constructor, there's no previous service call. In this case, passing null is expected.
The type name of the long-running operation making use of this class. Used when creating diagnostic scopes. If left null, the type name will be inferred based on the parameter .
The attributes to use during diagnostic scope creation.

member.name: M:Azure.Core.OperationInternalBase.WaitForCompletionResponseAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary:
Periodically calls until the long-running operation completes. After each service call, a retry-after header may be returned to communicate that there is no reason to poll for status change until the specified time has passed. The maximum of the retry after value and the fallback strategy is then used as the wait interval. Headers supported are: "Retry-After", "retry-after-ms", and "x-ms-retry-after-ms", Usage example:
public async ValueTask<Response<T>> WaitForCompletionAsync(CancellationToken cancellationToken) => await _operationInternal.WaitForCompletionAsync(cancellationToken).ConfigureAwait(false);
member.param: A controlling the request lifetime.
member.returns: The last HTTP response received from the server, including the final result of the long-running operation.
member.exception: Thrown if there's been any issues during the connection, or if the operation has completed with failures.

member.name: M:Azure.Core.OperationInternalBase.WaitForCompletionResponseAsync(System.TimeSpan,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary:
Periodically calls until the long-running operation completes. The interval between calls is defined by the parameter , but it can change based on information returned from the server. After each service call, a retry-after header may be returned to communicate that there is no reason to poll for status change until the specified time has passed. In this case, the maximum value between the parameter and the retry-after header is chosen as the wait interval. Headers supported are: "Retry-After", "retry-after-ms", and "x-ms-retry-after-ms". Usage example:
public async ValueTask<Response<T>> WaitForCompletionAsync(TimeSpan pollingInterval, CancellationToken cancellationToken) => await _operationInternal.WaitForCompletionAsync(pollingInterval, cancellationToken).ConfigureAwait(false);
member.param: The interval between status requests to the server.
A controlling the request lifetime.
member.returns: The last HTTP response received from the server, including the final result of the long-running operation.
member.exception: Thrown if there's been any issues during the connection, or if the operation has completed with failures.

member.name: M:Azure.Core.OperationInternal`1.#ctor(Azure.Core.IOperation{`0},Azure.Core.Pipeline.ClientDiagnostics,Azure.Response,System.String,System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.Collections.Generic.KeyValuePair{System.String,System.String}},Azure.Core.DelayStrategy)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: The long-running operation making use of this class. Passing "this" is expected.
Used for diagnostic scope and exception creation. This is expected to be the instance created during the construction of your main client.

The initial value of . Usually, long-running operation objects can be instantiated in two ways:
When calling a client's "Start<OperationName>" method, a service call is made to start the operation, and an instance is returned. In this case, the response received from this service call can be passed here.
When a user instantiates an directly using a public constructor, there's no previous service call. In this case, passing null is expected.
The type name of the long-running operation making use of this class. Used when creating diagnostic scopes. If left null, the type name will be inferred based on the parameter .
The attributes to use during diagnostic scope creation.
The delay strategy when Retry-After header is not present. When it is present, the longer of the two delays will be used. Default is .

member.name: P:Azure.Core.ForwardsClientCallsAttribute.SkipChecks
member.summary: Gets whether or not we should validate DiagnosticScope for this API. In the case where there is an internal API that makes the Azure API call and a public API that uses it we need ForwardsClientCalls. If the public API will cache the results then the diagnostic scope will not always be created because an Azure API is not always called. In this case we need to turn off this validation for this API only.
